reviews.review_msg: If you ever see this man as a professor for a class, please take it. I have only had him once and this breaks my heart. Honestly never had so much fun in a course. He made school fun

Amazing prof. Only rarely do you meet a prof willing to listen to your questions, concerns, issues related to completing your assignment with a genuine caring vibe. Always accommodated my problems to help me get the best grade I could achieve.

One of my favourite profs at SFU, the material was dry at times but he made lectures interesting.0 and fun He obviously cared about the material and his excitement was infectious, overall I would love to take another course with him.

This was my first class in college in 5 years and my first English course in nearly 10 years. I enjoyed most of the book selections and found Nathan to be a great professor. The lectures were okay, the assignments were good and the grading was fair. I didn't care for the weekly in-class on the spot writing, but it is a W course. Loved the project.

He is so passionate and knowledgeable about the subject that you can probably feel it during his lectures. After taking his course, I am confident to say I have known more about the topics we discussed and am very interested to pursue further studies in the same field. Also I would say he is a bit towards the lenient side in terms of grading.
the selection of books are still fine but his teaching is terrible and unbearable. Super boring for a night class. He does not seem to like his job or even the topic he is teaching.

Although his exams are pretty tough, he is a reasonable marker. He cares a lot about his students and is always willing to take questions after class. He keeps the class interesting by cracking a few dad jokes here and there. Overall, a super great guy!
